topic: Theme 3: Communication and the World Around Us
subject: french

items.q: What is the French word for 'Christmas'?
items.why: Students omit the diaeresis (tréma). Many festival words have crucial accents: Noël, fête, pâques, élève (student-related). Omitting accents in writing loses marks; the tréma indicates 'e' is pronounced separately.

items.q: How do you say 'I am going to visit Paris' (near future) in French?
items.why: Students add 'à' between aller and the infinitive. Futur proche structure is ALLER (conjugated) + INFINITIVE, with no preposition: 'je vais manger', 'je vais aller', 'je vais partir'.
A classic faux ami. Actuellement looks like 'actually' but means CURRENTLY. To say 'actually' use en fait or en réalité. These false friends regularly cost marks.

items.q: In which year did Algeria gain independence from France?
items.why: It's 1962. “1954” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which is NOT part of the Maghreb?
items.why: It's Sénégal. “Algérie” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was Quebec's Law 101 (Charter of the French Language) passed?
items.why: Exact answer: 1977. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
